summary: Backend (FastAPI /api/health) fully tested and passing 4/4 tests. Frontend (Expo) is NOT accessible via public URL https://idea-parking.preview.emergentagent.com due to a persistent ngrok tunnel failure. The Metro bundler starts but the ngrok tunnel keeps crashing with 'failed to start tunnel - endpoint already online' and 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading body)'. Supabase tables need to be manually created via the SQL editor using /app/supabase_setup.sql. Code review shows well-structured code with proper testIDs, RN components, and StyleSheet usage.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend completely inaccessible via https://idea-parking.preview.emergentagent.com - returns 'The preview environment is not responding'. Root cause: ngrok tunnel fails continuously with 'ERR_NGROK_334 - endpoint already online' and 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading body)'. Metro bundler starts but tunnel never establishes.

expo export --platform web fails with 'window is not defined' ReferenceError from @react-native-async-storage/async-storage during SSR. This is a known Expo web SSR issue with AsyncStorage. The Supabase client uses AsyncStorage which tries to access window during static export.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix ngrok tunnel - the tunnel endpoint 'idea-parking.ngrok.io' is already online and new connections fail. Possible fixes: (1) Kill existing ngrok process and restart, (2) Use ngrok --pooling-enabled flag, (3) Investigate if another process holds the ngrok session. This blocks ALL frontend testing.
IMPORTANT: Run /app/supabase_setup.sql in Supabase SQL editor to create tables (profiles, focuses, checkins, parked_ideas) before the app can function.

OPTIONAL: Fix web export SSR issue - wrap AsyncStorage / Supabase client initialization in a platform check or use expo-secure-store with a web fallback to prevent 'window is not defined' during static export.
Create test user testuser@noshift.app / TestPass123 via Supabase dashboard after SQL setup.
critical_code_review_comments: Code quality is good overall - proper testIDs on all interactive elements, RN components used correctly, StyleSheet.create() used throughout.
supabase.ts: Using AsyncStorage for session persistence which works in mobile/web dev mode but fails during static export (window is not defined). Not a runtime bug, only affects static export.
settings.tsx: Admin PIN (2847) is hardcoded in the frontend source - security concern for production. Consider moving to environment variable or server-side validation.

onboarding.tsx: Platform.OS check for DateTimePicker - showDatePicker defaults to Platform.OS === 'ios', which means on web it defaults to showing the iOS spinner which may not render correctly in web preview.
_layout.tsx: The hasOnboarded check queries focuses table on every auth state change - if the Supabase tables don't exist yet, this silently fails and always routes to onboarding.
seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Supabase tables not yet verified as created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is healthy. Frontend is blocked by ngrok tunnel issue. The Supabase SQL setup script at /app/supabase_setup.sql must be run before testing any data flows. Test user to create: testuser@noshift.app / TestPass123. Once ngrok is fixed, test: login->onboarding(6 steps)->home(checkin modal)->parking lot->progress->history->settings(logout). Admin panel PIN is 2847, accessible via long press on version text in settings.
rca_of_issue: Ngrok tunnel failure RCA: The ngrok agent at /app/frontend/node_modules/@expo/ngrok-bin-linux-arm64/ngrok tries to start a tunnel for the 'idea-parking' subdomain. Error ERR_NGROK_334 means the endpoint is already registered with ngrok's cloud. This happens when the previous container session's ngrok tunnel is still active/not cleaned up, or when the supervisor restarts expo too quickly. The tunnel process PID 8322 (ngrok start --none) is still running from a previous session, causing conflicts. Fix: Kill all existing ngrok processes (pkill -f ngrok), then restart expo via supervisorctl.
